title: scene-outliner
content:
Scene Outliner

The Scene panel (left side, toggle via ☰ Scene in the toolbar) shows the hierarchy of all loaded models and objects in the scene.

Creating objects

You can add parametric primitives directly in the outliner without importing a file.

  • + button (next to the search field) – opens a menu to create Box, Sphere, Cylinder, Cone, Plane, or Torus. The new object is added as a child of the currently selected object, or as a new root in the scene if nothing is selected.

  • Right-click on any object – use Add Box, Add Sphere, etc. to create a primitive as a child of that object.

  • Add empty Object3D – creates an empty grouping node (no visible geometry).

Default primitive size is unit scale (1×1×1). After creation, select the object and edit dimensions in the Selected panel under the Parametric folder.

Editing parametric objects

Parametric primitives store their dimensions in userData. When you change width, height, radius, or other parameters in the Selected panel, the mesh geometry is regenerated immediately.

Export to GLB preserves parametric metadata so objects can be re-imported and edited later in this application.

Other outliner actions

  • Drag and drop – reparent objects in the hierarchy.

  • Eye icon – toggle visibility.

  • Clone – duplicate an object (parametric clones get independent geometry and parameters).

  • Search – filter the tree by name (supports wildcards).
